seoTitle: How to Display Multiple Values in One Power BI Card
metaDescription: Combine several KPIs into a single Power BI card for a clean, consolidated display that's easier for stakeholders to read at a glance.
intro: Spreading every KPI across its own separate card can clutter a Power BI report, when combining related values into one card would communicate more clearly. This guide covers how to display multiple values together for a clean KPI presentation. It's a useful technique for sales, finance, and marketing dashboards where quick decision-making matters.

steps.heading: Prepare your KPI fields
steps.body: Identify the specific values you want to combine into a single card, making sure each one is ready in your data model.

steps.heading: Add values to the card
steps.body: Insert the multiple measures into the same card visual so they're grouped together in one consolidated view.

steps.heading: Customize the appearance
steps.body: Adjust labels, sizing, and spacing so each value is clearly distinguishable within the shared card.

steps.heading: Apply it to real use cases
steps.body: Use the combined card for scenarios like sales, finance, or marketing metrics where a compact, at-a-glance view is valuable.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for KPI cards
steps.body: Limit the number of values on a single card so it stays readable rather than becoming cluttered and hard to scan quickly.

faq.question: How many values can I fit on one card in Power BI?
faq.answer: There's no strict limit, but keeping it to a small number of closely related values keeps the card easy to read.

faq.question: Is combining values into one card a built-in Power BI feature?
faq.answer: Yes, it relies on standard card visual customization rather than needing a custom or third-party visual.

faq.question: Will this technique work for both sales and finance dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes, it's flexible enough to apply to any KPI set, including sales, finance, or marketing metrics.

faq.question: Can I update the values automatically as data refreshes?
faq.answer: Yes, since the card pulls from your live measures, the displayed values update automatically with your data refresh.

faq.question: Does this technique require advanced DAX knowledge?
faq.answer: Basic measure setup is usually enough, though more advanced KPI logic could involve some additional DAX depending on your data.
